mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.1% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, reducing corporate earnings growth and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) above 142,288k signal robust labor market demand, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,206k signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate weakening aggregate demand, reducing corporate earnings growth and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns in demand contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 238.099 combined with stable interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals persistent price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
